bids.reasoning: The opponent opened at t=0.00 with all valueC across every issue, which gives me the minimum utility (0.20 per issue) — a very low offer. Their message claims it's a "market standard" and their principal's limit, which is classic cheap talk at the very start. Since we're at t=0.00 with no time pressure, I should open with my best bid (all valueA) to maximize my utility and anchor high. My utility for all-A is 1.0 across all issues.

The opponent has made the same all-valueC bid twice, claiming they can't move beyond valueC on any issue. This is classic fabricated constraint/cheap talk - it's extremely early (t=0.02) and they've shown zero concession. Their offer gives me utility of 0.2 across all issues = 0.2 total, which is very low. I should hold firm near my ideal bid since we're at the very start of negotiations. I'll repeat my all-valueA bid as it's optimal for me, and there's plenty of time for the opponent to concede.

We're very early (t=0.04), so I should hold firm near my optimal bid. I'll keep offering all valueA for maximum utility of 1.0, as there's no reason to concede yet.
